chunks.text: Vasuki Narasimha Swamy and César Torres win Microsoft Research Dissertation Grants - EECS at Berkeley EE graduate student Vasuki Narasimha Swamy (adviser: Anant Sahai) and CS graduate student César Torres (adviser: Eric Paulos) have won inaugural Microsoft Research Dissertation Grants. These grants offer financial support to selected doctoral students from groups that are under-represented in the field of computing. Of the 200 applicants, only 12 were chosen. Vasuki’s research topic is “Real-time Ultra-reliable Wireless Communication” and César’s is “Hybrid Aesthetics – A New Media Framework for the Computational Design of Creative Materials, Tools, and Practices within Digital Fabrication.” Categories Honors Research Students Related Links Microsoft Research Blog: Dissertation Grant Winners Announced View Open Faculty Positions
